learning_outcomes.description: Understand and apply the key principles of sustainability and circularity
Use appropriate tools to assess sustainability on individual, process, product and global levels
Work in a team to design a circular product/process and make recommendations that consider design, technological, supply chain, social, political and business aspects
Find reliable data and analyse impacts on sustainability on variations of a circular product/process

assessments.task_description:
This assignment will focus on use of simple sustainability and circular economy principles and tools.

It is an individual assignment.

Full requirements are on blackboard>assessment folder.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Critical review of existing detailed sustainability or circular assessments.

It is an individual assignment.

Full requirements are on blackboard>assessment folder.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assignment 3 Circular product/process report and pitch
assessments.task_description:
Team report (20%) and team interview (10%) on a circular product. Includes a circular engineering product incorporating holistic approach to product design, sustainability analyses, business aspects, supply chains, and policy and social effects. 

This is a team based project. Team self assessments (in the contribution table and in buddycheck) and tutor observations will be used for assessing individual contributions.

Full requirements are on blackboard>assessment folder.

For the team report: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

For the team interview: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Final exam may have questions on circular economy principles, assessment tools, and a critical review of a detailed assessment example.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
